title: campylospermum malabaricum
intro: Campylospermum malabaricum, also known as the Malabar milk bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to Western Ghats in India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Campylospermum malabaricum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural laxative, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Campylospermum malabaricum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are commonly consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
